name: VICTOR
description: International phase 2 randomised controlled trial comparing venetoclax-low dose cytarabine with standard intensive chemotherapy in newly diagnosed AML
weblinkText: A trial of venetoclax with low dose cytarabine for acute myeloid leukaemia (VICTOR)
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥50
Fit for intensive therapy
NPM1 mutated FLT3-ITD negative
CD33 positive acute myeloid leukemia
exclusionCriteria: Receipt of any previous therapy for AML or any antecedent haematological condition (the use of oral hydroxycarbamide to control white blood cell count is permitted)
Other active malignancy requiring treatment

name: OPTIMISE-FLT3
description: International phase 3 randomised controlled trial comparing intensive treatment strategies for newly diagnosed FLT3-mutated AML
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥16
Fit for intensive therapy
FLT3-ITD or FLT3-TKD mutated
New presentations requiring emergency treatment can be enrolled prior to FLT3 result
exclusionCriteria: Receipt of any previous therapy for AML or any antecedent haematological condition (the use of oral hydroxycarbamide to control white blood cell count is permitted)
Other active malignancy requiring treatment
Blast transformation of chronic myeloid leukaemia (CML)

name: EVOLVE-1
description: International phase 3 randomised trial of newly diagnosed patients ineligible for intensive chemotherapy with IDH1m AML using ivosidenib plus azacitidine ± venetoclax.
inclusionCriteria: Patients with newly diagnosed IDH1-mutated AML or MDS/AML according to the 2022 International Consensus Classification
Age ≥ 18 years, no upper age limit
Ineligible for intensive induction chemotherapy by meeting at least 1 of the following criteria:
≥ 75 years of age ineligible for intensive chemotherapy per physician’s discretion (ECOG PS 0-2)
18-74 years: not eligible for standard chemotherapy with any of the following co-morbidities:
ECOG PS 2 or 3
Cardiac history of chronic heart failure requiring treatment; or with an ejection fraction ≤50%; or chronic stable angina
DLCO ≤ 65% or FEV1 ≤ 65%
Creatinine clearance ≥ 30 mL/min to <45 ml/min calculated by the Cockcroft Gault formula
Moderate hepatic impairment with total bilirubin > 1.5 to < 3.0 x upper limit of normal (ULN)
exclusionCriteria: Previous AML treatment except hydroxyurea to control WBC counts. ESA or luspatercept for MDS is allowed. Prior hypomethylating agent for MDS-EB is not allowed.
AML with BCR-ABL1; or myeloid blast crisis of CML

name: EVOLVE-2
description: International phase 3 randomised trial of newly diagnosed patients ineligible for intensive chemotherapy with either NPM1m or KMT2Ar AML 1:1 to venetoclax plus-azacitidine ± revumenib (menin inhibitor).
inclusionCriteria: Patients with newly diagnosed NPM1-mutated or KMT2A-rearranged AML or MDS/AML according to the 2022 International Consensus Classification
Age ≥ 18 years, no upper age limit
Ineligible for intensive induction chemotherapy by meeting at least 1 of the following criteria:
≥ 75 years of age ineligible for intensive chemotherapy per physician’s discretion (ECOG PS 0-2)
18-74 years: not eligible for standard chemotherapy with any of the following co-morbidities:
ECOG PS 2 or 3
Cardiac history of chronic heart failure requiring treatment; or with an ejection fraction ≤50%; or chronic stable angina
DLCO ≤ 65% or FEV1 ≤ 65%
Creatinine clearance ≥ 30 mL/min to <45 ml/min calculated by the Cockcroft Gault formula
Moderate hepatic impairment with total bilirubin > 1.5 to < 3.0 x upper limit of normal (ULN)
exclusionCriteria: Previous AML treatment except hydroxyurea to control WBC counts. ESA or luspatercept for MDS is allowed. Prior hypomethylating agent for MDS-EB is not allowed.
AML with BCR-ABL1; or myeloid blast crisis of CML

name: AZTOUND
description: Phase 2 trial assessing oral azacitidine and venetoclax in newly diagnosed AML unfit for intensive therapy
inclusionCriteria: Age >75 or <75 with comorbidity/ECOG 2

name: KOMEN-017
description: International phase 3 randomised placebo-controlled trial assessing ziftomenib in combination with intensive therapy or Venetoclax-Azacitidine in patients with untreated NPM1 mutated or KMT2A-rearranged acute myeloid leukaemia
inclusionCriteria: Patients with newly diagnosed NPM1-mutated or KMT2A-rearranged AML
Intensive arm: Newly diagnosed NPM1-mutated or KMT2A-rearranged AML
Non-intensive arm: Newly diagnosed NPM1-mutated AML
sites: Barts, Guys & St Thomas', Leeds, St George's, Wolverhampton

name: CAMELOT-2
description: Phase 3 Randomised, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Study of Bleximenib, Venetoclax and Azacitidine for with untreated NPM1 mutated or KMT2A-rearranged acute myeloid leukaemia ineligible for Intensive Chemotherapy
inclusionCriteria: Patients with newly diagnosed NPM1-mutated or KMT2A-rearranged AML Ineligible for Intensive Chemotherapy
sites: Brighton, Cambridge, Canterbury, Colchester, Edinburgh, Liverpool, Guys & St Thomas', Plymouth, Worthing

name: QUANTUM-WILD
description: Phase 3, Double-Blind, Randomised, Placebo-Controlled Trial Of Quizartinib or Placebo Plus Chemotherapy in Newly Diagnosed Patients With FLT3-ITD Negative AML
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥ 18 years and ≤70
Newly diagnosed, morphologically documented primary AML based on World Health Organization (WHO) 2016 classification
ECOG performance status 0-2
Patient fit for standard “7+3” induction chemotherapy regimen
exclusionCriteria: Acute promyelocytic leukemia (APL), or BCR-ABL positive leukaemia
AML secondary to prior chemotherapy or radiotherapy
AML with known antecedent MDS, MPN or MDS/MPNs including chronic myelomonocytic leukemia (CMML), atypical chronic myeloid leukemia (aCML), juvenile myelomonocytic leukemia (JMML) and others
Newly diagnosed AML with FLT3-ITD mutations ≥5% VAF (or ≥0.05 SR) based on a validated FLT3 mutation assay
sites: Queen Elizabeth Hospital Birmingham, King's College Hospital, Hammersmith Hospital, Maidstone, Manchester Royal Infirmary, Oxford, Torquay

name: CAMELOT-1
description: Phase 1/2 study of the menin inhibitor bleximenib in relapsed/refractory (R/R) NPM1 mutated or KMT2A rearranged AML
inclusionCriteria: R/R AML with either NPM1-mutation or KMT2A-rearrangement
sites: Cardiff, The Christie, Liverpool, Guys & St Thomas', Leeds, Oxford, Plymouth, UCLH

name: DSP-5336
description: Phase 1/2 study of the menin inhibitor enzomenib in relapsed/refractory (R/R) NPM1 mutated or KMT2A rearranged AML
inclusionCriteria: R/R AML blasts >5% with NPM1m, KMT2Ar, TP53m, NUP98, UBTF-TD
sites: Queen Elizabeth Hospital Birmingham, The Christie, Oxford, Kings' College Hospital, Guys & St Thomas' Hospital

name: APL0498
description: Phase 1 study to investigate the safety and efficacy of APL-4098 alone, and in combination with azacitidine or azacitidine and venetoclax in AML and MDS/AML
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥18

R/R AML (primary or secondary, including treatment-related), participant is intolerant to, or considered ineligible for available therapies known to provide clinical benefit. Must have ≥5% blasts in BM or ≥1% blasts in the peripheral blood.
MDS/AML defined as diagnosis of MDS with 10-19% BM blasts who have not had a response after at least four cycles of treatment with a monotherapy hypomethylating agent (HMA)
MDS with 5%-9% bone marrow blasts who are not benefiting with monotherapy hypomethylating agents (Phase 1 only; MDS-EB patients will not be enrolled in the Phase 2 part of the study)
exclusionCriteria: Allogeneic transplant within 6 months of screening
sites: Cardiff, The Royal Marsden, Guys & St Thomas'

name: JNJ-89853413
description: Phase 1/2 study of a CD33xVδ2 T-Cell Engager for Relapsed or Refractory Acute Myeloid Leukemia or Myelodysplastic Neoplasms
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥18
Relapsed/refractory acute myeloid leukemia (AML) or relapsed/refractory moderate high, high, or very high risk IPSS-M myelodysplastic neoplasms (MDS)
eGFR ≥40 mL/min, ECOG 0-2
exclusionCriteria: Corticosteroids >10 mg daily prednisone equivalents, autoimmune disease that requires systemic immunosuppressive medications
AlloHSCT within 3 months
Uncontrolled systemic viral, bacterial, or fungal infection
Active CNS involvement

name: MOMENTUM
description: Phase 1/2 study of AZD3632 Monotherapy or in Combination With Anticancer Agents in Participants With Advanced Haematologic Malignancies With KMT2Ar, NPM1m, or Other Genotypes Associated With HOX Overexpression
inclusionCriteria: R/R AML blasts >5% with NPM1m, KMT2Ar, KMT2A-PTD, other menin-dependent mutations/translocations
exclusionCriteria: Active CNS involvement

name: BioDriveAFS
description: BioDrive seeks to identify the most effective way to prevent and detect fungal infection in patients with acute leukaemia (acute myeloid leukaemia, acute lymphoblastic leukaemia, high-risk myelodysplastic syndromes and transformed myeloproliferative neoplasms); assessing if monitoring the risk of fungal infection by regular blood tests is safe and reduces the need to use antifungal drugs.
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥16 years

Diagnosis of new, or relapsed, acute leukaemia or haematological disorder judged to need chemotherapy by the patient’s clinical care team, including AML, ALL, HRMDS, or AML transformation of a myeloproliferative neoplasm (tMPN)

Patient expected to have prolonged neutropenia related to chemotherapy which would mandate either antifungal prophylaxis and/or systematic invasive fungal infection biomarker monitoring (at least weekly)
exclusionCriteria: Previous proven or probable invasive fungal infection (IFI) (according to the EORTC/MSG criteria)
Contraindication to all potential prophylactic antifungal agents
Planned chemotherapy using any regimen that mandates the use of systemic antifungal medication

name: PROPEL
description: PROPEL is a multicentre randomised controlled trial comparing best practice usual care (BPUC) with a personalised prehabilitation care package (PPCP) incorporating a 12-month internal pilot, parallel process evaluation and economic evaluation.
inclusionCriteria: Age ≥ 16 years, treated on adult AML pathway
Diagnosis of either AML or MDS-EB2 (MDS with ≥10% blasts in the bone marrow) following first diagnosis or disease relapse
In complete morphological remission (defined <5% blasts in bone marrow)
Planned to receive either: Intensive treatment (e.g. anthracycline/cytarabine based chemotherapy and/or HSCT), or lower intensity treatment (i.e. Venetoclax based treatment) with HSCT – Pathway 1
Or: Planned to receive lower intensity treatment without HSCT (e.g. Venetoclax or Ivosidenib based treatment) - Pathway 2
Prior to, or within the 1st course of treatment following remission
Planned to receive at least one further full cycle of treatment (chemotherapy or HSCT) post randomisation
Access to the internet and an email address
Willing to use videoconferencing to undertake the appointments and sessions
exclusionCriteria: Diagnosis of Acute Promyelocytic Leukaemia
Undergoing single agent Azacitidine, single agent low dose Cytarabine, Menin inhibitors or FLT3 inhibitor treatment without planned HSCT

name: MO-TRANS
description: MO-TRANS is a randomised multi-centre phase 3 trial of mocravimod maintenance treatment post allogeneic stem cell transplant
inclusionCriteria: AML according to WHO 2022 classification
ELN high-risk or intermediate-risk in CR1, any risk AML in CR2
Planned allogeneic transplant from 9/10-10/10 HLA-matched donor or haploidentical donor with use of PBSC graft
Any conditioning regime with a Transplant conditioning Score (TCI) ≥ 1.5
Use of Tacrolimus-based GVHD prophylaxis
Age ≥18 and ≤75
exclusionCriteria: Use of ciclosporin, anti-thymocyte globulin (ATG), alemtuzumab or abatacept for GVHD prophylaxis
Macular oedema
sites: Queen Elizabeth Hospital Birmingham, Cardiff, Glasgow, Imperial College London, Newcastle, Oxford, Sheffield

name: GRACE
description: Phase 1/2 study assessing granulocyte augmentation to single-cord blood transplantation for high risk AML
inclusionCriteria: Fit, transplant-eligible patients, aged <55
High risk AML or MDS/AML (as defined by ICC 2022) fitting one or more of the following criteria:
TP53 mutation (single- or multi-hit)
Presence of inv(3)(q21.3q26.2) or t(3;3)(q21.3;q26.2)
Adverse risk (as per ICC 2022) and >0.1% MRD by flow cytometry after 2 cycles of induction
AML (any risk) with partial remission (<10% blasts) after 2 cycles induction
Early relapse (<6 months) after chemotherapy alone (excluding t(16;16), inv(16) or t(8;21))
Bone marrow performed within 28 days of starting conditioning chemotherapy demonstrates either: <10% blasts; or >10% blasts with a hypocellular background
FEV1 >65% without dyspnoea on mild activity
AST/ALT <3 x ULN, Bilirubin <1.5 x ULN (excluding Gilbert’s syndrome)
Performance Status (ECOG) of 0 or 1
exclusionCriteria: AML secondary to a myeloproliferative neoplasm
Active CNS disease
Prior allogeneic stem cell transplant
sites: Kings' College Hospital, The Christie, The Royal Marsden
